LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS RYAN CANSLER AND KELLY CORWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 19, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6C AND THE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPERATIVE PLEADING AND THE OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MARCH 7, 2014 AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS RYAN CANSLER AND KELLY CORWELL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 19, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6C AND THE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPERATIVE PLEADING AND THE OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MARCH 7, 2014 AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS RYAN CANSLER AND KELLY CORWELL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 2, 2014 TO GIVE DEFENDANTS THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B AND 2.6C. DEFENDANTS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS AND THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 5, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE DEMURRER WILL BE OVERRULED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS ONLY. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

Off Calendar May-16-2014 Continued To Jul-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHNNY D. KNADLER, TEL. NO. 948-3571 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RYAN CANSLER AND KELLY CORWELL'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is Denied. The First Amended Complaint pleads that the Defendant "appeared to have previously consumed alcoholic beverages before knowingly driving the vehicle" and that the Defendant appeared "under the influence." Accepting these facts as true, this is sufficient to support a prayer for punitive damages. (See Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHNNY D. KNADLER, TEL. NO. 948-3571 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RYAN CANSLER AND KELLY CORWELL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Overruled. "If the complaint states a cause of action under any theory, regardless of the title under which the factual basis for relief is stated, that aspect of the complaint is good against a demurrer." (Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38.) The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads a cause of action for battery. The element of intent may be satisfied if the evidence shows the defendant acted with a willful disregard of the plaintiff's rights. (Ashcraft v. King (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 605, 613.) The FAC pleads that the Defendant "knew or should have known the light was red" but did not stop at the light because he was distracted by his cellphone and was under the influence. Accepting all facts pled as true, this is sufficient to establish the intent element. The FAC is not so uncertain that Defendant cannot reasonably respond. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hunter Sims, Esq., 510.457.3468, for Defendants Ryan Cansler and Kelly Corwell; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RYAN CANSLER and KELLY CORWELL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for intentional tort if plaintiff can in good faith allege willful disregard of his rights. Accepting as true the allegations in the Complaint establishes only that Defendant illegally ran a red light and hit Plaintiff's vehicle; they do not establish Defendant's state of mind or intent, or of Defendant's awareness either that the light was red or of the likelihood of colliding with another vehicle even if he was aware. In addition, the third cause of action is vague in that it does not distinguish which "Defendant" is the subject of the description in para. IT-1. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hunter Sims, Esq., 510.457.3468, for Defendants Ryan Cansler and Kelly Corwell; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RYAN CANSLER and KELLY CORWELL'S
Motion to Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Complaint is granted with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff alleges only that Defendant had a red light but failed to stop, proceeded into the intersection, and struck Plaintiff's vehicle. There is no allegation that Defendant intentionally failed to stop, that he knew the light was red but nonetheless proceeded through the intersection, or that he intended to strike Plaintiff's vehicle. The complaint lacks allegations of actual intent or facts establishing Defendant's awareness of dangerous consequences and a deliberate failure to avoid them. Moreover, the Complaint does not allege facts that establish despicable conduct on the part of Defendant(s). Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
